verses.text: Set the trumpet to the mouth: they have broken the covenant and 'cast off the thing that is good.' Alarm sounded. To despise what is good is to invite the judgment of God.
Set the trumpet to the mouth: they have broken the covenant and 'cast off the thing that is good.' Alarm sounded. To despise what is good is to invite the judgment of God.
Set the trumpet to the mouth: they have broken the covenant and 'cast off the thing that is good.' Alarm sounded. To despise what is good is to invite the judgment of God.
They set up kings 'not by me,' and made idols of their silver; 'the calf of Samaria' shall be broken. Self-willed religion. Worship of man's own making cannot stand.
They set up kings 'not by me,' and made idols of their silver; 'the calf of Samaria' shall be broken. Self-willed religion. Worship of man's own making cannot stand.
They set up kings 'not by me,' and made idols of their silver; 'the calf of Samaria' shall be broken. Self-willed religion. Worship of man's own making cannot stand.
'They have sown the wind, and they shall reap the whirlwind'; Israel is swallowed among the nations. Harvest of folly. Empty deeds breed a devastating return.
'They have sown the wind, and they shall reap the whirlwind'; Israel is swallowed among the nations. Harvest of folly. Empty deeds breed a devastating return.
They hired lovers among the nations; now God will gather them, and they shall sorrow. Bought friendships. Alliances purchased against God end in grief.
They hired lovers among the nations; now God will gather them, and they shall sorrow. Bought friendships. Alliances purchased against God end in grief.
Ephraim multiplied altars to sin; God wrote 'the great things of my law,' but they counted them 'a strange thing.' Scripture despised. Familiar truth, unheeded, becomes foreign to the heart.
Ephraim multiplied altars to sin; God wrote 'the great things of my law,' but they counted them 'a strange thing.' Scripture despised. Familiar truth, unheeded, becomes foreign to the heart.
They sacrifice, but God accepts it not, for 'Israel hath forgotten his Maker'; He will send fire on the cities. Forgotten God. Worship from a forgetful heart cannot please Him.
They sacrifice, but God accepts it not, for 'Israel hath forgotten his Maker'; He will send fire on the cities. Forgotten God. Worship from a forgetful heart cannot please Him.
